title: California Assembly Committee Considers SB 237: LCFS Credit Price Cap, Fuel Blend Overhaul, and â€œOne-Stop Shopâ€ Refinery Permitting Gain Traction
content:
On 23rd June, 2025, California assembly committee considered SB 237 . The bill proposes a cap on LCFS credit pricesâ€”set to the average market rate as of January 1, 2025, with future increases tied to inflation. The bill requires CARB to adopt these changes as emergency regulations exempt from traditional review processes, valid until January 1, 2031. The legislation also mandates standardized regulatory impact analyses for all CARB or Energy Commission rules that affect retail fuel prices.
A major structural change includes suspending Californiaâ€™s boutique gasoline specification (Section 2262.4) from June 2025 through October 2030, while directing state agencies to evaluate and recommend a West-wide gasoline standard by July 2026, in coordination with other western states. This aims to reduce refinery exit risk and price volatility.

Further, SB 237 introduces a â€œOne-Stop Shopâ€ environmental permitting process for refineries. In exchange for a binding commitment to supply affordable transportation fuels, refiners would receive coordinated review of all environmental permits. The bill outlines detailed timelines, transparency, and public engagement requirements, while preserving the authority of individual agencies.
author: Analysts Account
